role_summary: Civil Rights History Project interviewee; the LoC oral history is the primary archival source for this catalog page.
ai_reading: Myrtle Gonza Glascoe's neighbor counts are moderate (peak 86), with Charles McLaurin (entry 17) as the top match and the broader cluster including Harold K. Brown (#55), Gwendolyn Zoharah Simmons (born Gwen Robinson) (#53), John Churchville (#63).
biographical_paragraph: Myrtle Gonza Glascoe's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 137 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. The LoC transcript carries an ingestion-only audit tier: the catalog ingested the transcript but did not subject it to the audit cascade, and any specific biographical or quoted claims should be confirmed against external archives before citation. Independent biographical sourcing for an extended orientation paragraph is pending; the strongest signal currently available is the embedding pattern named in the AI's reading above.
